milestone: / (blind engine pass + scored, §34-gated) — contamination-resistant held-out repo

repo.name: kagent-dev/kagent
repo.stack: Go (CNCF k8s agent controller; 3 modules adk/api/core)

run.engine_proxy: blind-workflow (curator≠blind≠scorer), doc-removed tree, Graphify structural path

reference_claims.claim: The controller HTTP server listens on port 8083 and is the entry point that receives the UI's A2A JSON-RPC requests.
reference_claims.currency_reason: Port 8083 is the controller URL across translator, CLI port-forward (cli/internal/cli/agent/utils.go:53), and golden test outputs.

reference_claims.claim: The controller proxies the A2A JSON-RPC request to the agent pod (it does not run the LLM itself).
reference_claims.currency_reason: The A2A proxy is wired as a PathPrefix handler delegating to the A2AHandlerMux; the controller forwards rather than invoking the model.

reference_claims.claim: The controller reaches the agent pod via its Kubernetes Service URL of the form http://{agent-name}.{namespace}:8080.
reference_claims.currency_reason: Agent pod URL is constructed as {name}.{namespace}:8080 in the translator and the agent-card URL; deployments expose container port 8080.

reference_claims.claim: The database holds these tables/models: Agent, ToolServer, Tool, Session, Task, Event, Feedback, and Memory (with a pgvector embedding and ExpiresAt).
reference_claims.currency_reason: All eight models exist in models.go with the documented field shapes; Memory uses pgvector.Vector and an ExpiresAt pointer.

reference_claims.claim: The Session model carries a Source column ('user' or 'agent') indicating how the session was created; agent-sourced sessions are excluded from the agent's session list.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/database/models.go:60-76 (SessionSourceUser/SessionSourceAgent, Session.Source); go/core/internal/database/queries/sessions.sql:14 (AND (source IS NULL OR source != 'agent'))
reference_claims.currency_reason: Source enum and column exist; ListSessionsForAgent SQL filters source != 'agent', matching the a2a-subagents session-query table.

reference_claims.claim: The controller manager runs multiple controllers (AgentController, RemoteMCPServerController, an MCPServer/MCPServerTool controller) that share a single reconciler instance.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The named controllers exist; the doc's box 'MCPServer Controller' maps to MCPServerToolController plus the ReconcileKagentMCPServer path. Minor naming drift only.

reference_claims.claim: The shared kagentReconciler holds the ADK translator, the kube client, and the DB client and is the single instance all controllers delegate to.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/core/internal/controller/reconciler/reconciler.go:83-90 (kagentReconciler struct: adkTranslator, kube, dbClient); :115-118 (NewKagentReconciler wires them)
reference_claims.currency_reason: The reconciler struct has exactly adkTranslator/kube/dbClient fields as the diagram shows.

reference_claims.claim: DB writes for agents and tool servers use atomic upserts (SQL INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE), making concurrent reconciliation idempotent without application-level locks.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/core/internal/database/queries/agents.sql:14 (ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE); go/core/internal/database/queries/tools.sql:41 (ON CONFLICT (name, group_kind) DO UPDATE); go/core/internal/controller/reconciler/reconciler.go:45 (no mutexes in reconciler)
reference_claims.currency_reason: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE upserts exist for agents/sessions/tasks/tools; the reconciler uses no Go sync primitives. (The doc attributes serialization to SQLite, but the upsert mechanism itself is real on Postgres.)

reference_claims.claim: Tool refresh wraps delete-all-existing + insert-new in a single DB transaction for atomicity.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/core/internal/database/client_postgres.go:358-360 (RefreshToolsForServer calls withTx); :34-43 (withTx Begin/Commit); SoftDeleteToolsForServer + inserts inside the tx
reference_claims.currency_reason: RefreshToolsForServer runs SoftDeleteToolsForServer plus inserts inside withTx (Begin/Commit), matching the documented transaction.

reference_claims.claim: Network I/O (connecting to remote MCP servers and listing tools) happens OUTSIDE database transactions so long-running network ops do not hold DB locks.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/core/internal/controller/reconciler/reconciler.go:1073-1097 (StoreToolServer upsert, then createMcpTransport+listTools with no tx, then RefreshToolsForServer transaction last)
reference_claims.currency_reason: upsertToolServerForRemoteMCPServer does the upsert, then the MCP connect + listTools (network, no tx), then RefreshToolsForServer (the only tx) — exactly the documented ordering.

reference_claims.claim: The AgentController's event predicate processes all Create and Delete events, but only processes Update events when the agent's generation or labels changed (filtering out status-only updates).
reference_claims.currency_reason: The For() predicate is GenerationChangedPredicate OR LabelChangedPredicate, exactly the documented update filtering.

reference_claims.claim: The Agent CRD (kagent.dev/v1alpha2) is the central CRD with type Declarative|BYO and references ModelConfig, RemoteMCPServer, and MCPServer.
reference_claims.currency_reason: AgentType enum and the Declarative/BYO sub-specs exist in agent_types.go under the v1alpha2 group.

reference_claims.claim: Agent CRD CEL validation enforces: type must be Declarative or BYO; the matching sub-spec must be set; systemMessage and systemMessageFrom are mutually exclusive; serviceAccountName and serviceAccountConfig are mutually exclusive.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/v1alpha2/agent_types.go:51-52 (type + declarative/byo XValidation); :168 (systemMessage/systemMessageFrom mutually exclusive); :422 (serviceAccountName/serviceAccountConfig mutually exclusive)
reference_claims.currency_reason: All four documented CEL XValidation rules are present at the cited lines.

reference_claims.claim: On an Agent's McpServerTool, each requireApproval entry must also appear in toolNames (CEL-enforced subset rule).
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/v1alpha2/agent_types.go:533 (XValidation: each RequireApproval entry must also appear in ToolNames)
reference_claims.currency_reason: The subset CEL rule is present on McpServerTool exactly as documented.

reference_claims.claim: The ModelConfig CRD configures LLM provider credentials/params, with provider an enum over Anthropic/OpenAI/AzureOpenAI/Ollama/Gemini/GeminiVertexAI/AnthropicVertexAI/Bedrock (plus SAPAICore).
reference_claims.currency_reason: The provider enum exists; the doc lists 8 providers but the code adds a 9th (SAPAICore). The structural claim holds; the enumeration is slightly under-counted in the doc but not contradicted.

reference_claims.claim: ModelConfig CEL rules enforce: provider-specific config blocks must only be set when provider matches; apiKeyPassthrough and apiKeySecret are mutually exclusive; apiKeyPassthrough is disallowed for Gemini/GeminiVertexAI/AnthropicVertexAI; TLS caCertSecretRef and caCertSecretKey must be set together.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/v1alpha2/modelconfig_types.go:371-379 (provider.X must be nil if provider != X); :382 (passthrough/apiKeySecret exclusive); :383 (passthrough false for Gemini/VertexAI); :318-319 (caCert ref/key require each other)
reference_claims.currency_reason: Each documented ModelConfig validation rule is present at the cited XValidation lines.

reference_claims.claim: The RemoteMCPServer CRD declares a remote MCP tool server with a protocol enum SSE|STREAMABLE_HTTP (default STREAMABLE_HTTP), url, headersFrom, terminateOnClose, and allowedNamespaces; its status carries discoveredTools.
reference_claims.currency_reason: All listed spec/status fields and the protocol enum+default exist in remotemcpserver_types.go.

reference_claims.claim: On RemoteMCPServer reconcile, the controller connects to the MCP server, lists tools, and populates status.discoveredTools.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The reconcile path connects (createMcpTransport), lists tools (listTools), and writes DiscoveredTools onto status.

reference_claims.claim: Common types include ValueRef (resolves a value from a Secret/ConfigMap key), AllowedNamespaces (From All|Selector, follows Gateway API pattern), and TypedReference/TypedLocalReference.
reference_claims.currency_reason: ValueRef, AllowedNamespaces with From=All/Selector, and the typed-reference types exist. (Doc names the enum AllowedNamespacesFrom; code names it FromNamespaces — prose drift only.)

reference_claims.claim: The translator (adk_api_translator.go, method translateInlineAgent) converts CRD types into Go ADK config (AgentConfig) which is serialized to config.json.
reference_claims.currency_reason: translateInlineAgent exists on adkApiTranslator and is the CRD->AgentConfig conversion entry; the doc cites adk_api_translator.go but the method lives in the sibling compiler.go of the same package (path-precise but file slightly off).

reference_claims.claim: The same config data flows through three type systems: CRD Go types -> Go ADK types (go/api/adk/types.go: AgentConfig, HttpMcpServerConfig, SseMcpServerConfig) -> Python ADK types, serialized as config.json.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The Go ADK AgentConfig/HttpMcpServerConfig/SseMcpServerConfig types exist (the doc cites go/adk/types.go but the real path is go/api/adk/types.go — path drift, content correct).

reference_claims.claim: Agent config is delivered by baking the resolved AgentConfig JSON into a Kubernetes Secret mounted at /config, and the agent pod reads /config/config.json at startup.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The translator builds a config Secret mounted at /config; the Python ADK CLI opens /config/config.json at startup.

reference_claims.claim: Prompt templates are resolved at reconciliation time using Go text/template with an include('source/key') function; the resolved plain-string prompt is stored in the config Secret. systemMessage is treated as a template only when promptTemplate is set.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/core/internal/controller/translator/agent/template.go:97-122 (executeSystemMessageTemplate, include funcMap, text/template); go/core/internal/controller/translator/agent/compiler.go:361-369 (only resolved when PromptTemplate set)
reference_claims.currency_reason: executeSystemMessageTemplate uses text/template with an include func; compiler.go gates resolution on spec.Declarative.PromptTemplate being set.

reference_claims.claim: When promptTemplate is set, template variables AgentName, AgentNamespace, Description, ToolNames, and SkillNames are available, populated after tools are translated.
reference_claims.currency_reason: PromptTemplateContext has exactly the five documented fields, built after the config (tools) is assembled.

reference_claims.claim: Kagent ships a kagent-builtin-prompts ConfigMap (via Helm) with keys skills-usage, tool-usage-best-practices, safety-guardrails, kubernetes-context, and a2a-communication.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The Helm ConfigMap defines all five documented keys.

reference_claims.claim: The agent controller watches ConfigMaps referenced by agents (via promptTemplate.dataSources / systemMessageFrom) and re-reconciles referencing agents when a ConfigMap changes.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The controller registers a ConfigMap watch keyed by referencesConfigMap to find and re-reconcile dependent agents.

reference_claims.claim: HITL tool approval is implemented via a before_tool_callback that calls ADK's ToolContext.request_confirmation() for tools in the requireApproval set; on resume the same tool call is replayed (no LLM call on the approve path).
reference_claims.code_anchor: python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/_approval.py:1-3,49-86 (before_tool_callback requests confirmation for specified tools via request_confirmation())
reference_claims.currency_reason: _approval.py implements the before_tool_callback that calls tool_context.request_confirmation() for the configured approval set, as documented.

reference_claims.claim: KAgentRemoteA2ATool (a BaseTool) replaces upstream AgentTool(RemoteA2aAgent) to manage the A2A subagent conversation and propagate HITL state up via request_confirmation(), with a two-phase run_async (normal call / HITL resume).
reference_claims.currency_reason: KAgentRemoteA2ATool exists as a BaseTool with the documented two-phase run_async and request_confirmation HITL propagation.

reference_claims.claim: KAgentRemoteA2AToolset is a thin BaseToolset wrapper whose job is closing the owned httpx.AsyncClient when the runner shuts down (ADK cleanup only discovers BaseToolset instances).
reference_claims.currency_reason: The toolset wrapper exists as a BaseToolset that owns/closes the httpx.AsyncClient, exactly as documented.

reference_claims.claim: _SubagentInterceptor (registered at A2A client construction) injects x-user-id (parent session user ID) and x-kagent-source: 'agent' headers on every outgoing subagent request; interceptors must be passed at ClientFactory.create time.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The interceptor injects both documented headers (source value 'agent') and is passed via interceptors=[...] at client construction.

reference_claims.claim: The A2aAgentExecutor builds a {tool_name -> session_id} map from tools implementing SubagentSessionProvider so the UI can show live subagent activity; the event converter stamps this as kagent_subagent_session_id metadata on function_call DataParts.
reference_claims.code_anchor: python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/_agent_executor.py:96 (class A2aAgentExecutor), :592-595 (builds map from SubagentSessionProvider tools); converters/event_converter.py:137 (stamps subagent session id onto function_call DataPart)
reference_claims.currency_reason: A2aAgentExecutor builds the SubagentSessionProvider map and the event converter stamps the subagent session id, as documented.

reference_claims.claim: The ADK->A2A event converter maps text to TextPart, FunctionCall/FunctionResponse to DataParts, errors to TaskState.failed, and sets task state working/completed/input_required/auth_required.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The converter sets the documented TaskState values for error/normal/auth/HITL events.

reference_claims.claim: A built-in ask_user tool is added unconditionally to every agent, letting the model pose batched questions, and it reuses the same request_confirmation HITL plumbing.
reference_claims.currency_reason: AskUserTool is appended unconditionally in types.py with the comment 'every agent can ask the user questions', reusing the HITL plumbing.

reference_claims.claim: Session queries differ by subagent visibility: GET /api/sessions/agent/{ns}/{name} excludes subagent (source='agent') sessions, while GET /api/sessions/{id} and GET /api/sessions/{id}/tasks include them.
reference_claims.currency_reason: The three documented session routes are registered and the agent-scoped list applies the source != 'agent' SQL filter.

reference_claims.claim: On template-resolution failure the agent's Accepted status condition is set to False (ReconcileFailed) and the deployment is not updated until resolved.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/v1alpha2/agent_types.go:90-95 (AgentStatus conditions Accepted/Ready); go/core/internal/controller/reconciler/reconciler.go:277 (reconcileAgentStatus surfaces reconcile error onto Accepted condition)
reference_claims.currency_reason: AgentStatus carries the Accepted condition and the reconciler funnels reconcile errors (including template-resolution failures from translateInlineAgent) into that condition.

reference_claims.claim: The executeCodeBlocks field on the declarative agent spec is currently ignored.
reference_claims.code_anchor: go/api/v1alpha2/agent_types.go:220-221 (comment: due to a bug in adk ... this field is ignored for now; ExecuteCodeBlocks *bool)
reference_claims.currency_reason: The field exists with an explicit 'ignored for now' comment, matching the doc.

emitted_claims.claim: kagent is a Go (1.26.3) project organized as a multi-module workspace under module path github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/go, split into three modules: adk, api, and core.

The controller binary entry point is core/cmd/controller/main.go; it constructs a NoopAuthorizer, selects an authenticator by auth mode (unsecure | trusted-proxy), wires the default agentsxk8s sandbox backend, and delegates the full startup to app.Start.

core/pkg/app.Start is the controller's central bootstrap/wiring routine: it parses flags+env config, initializes tracing, builds the controller-runtime Manager, runs DB migrations, connects to Postgres, constructs the reconciler/translator, registers every controller, and starts the HTTP + A2A + admin servers under the manager.

Controller startup workflow (ordered): SetupSignalHandler -> SetFlags+flag.Parse -> LoadFromEnv (env overrides flags) -> InitTracerProvider -> NewManager -> ResolveURL -> run DB migrations -> Connect+NewClient -> getExtensionConfig -> build translator+reconciler -> SetupWithManager for each controller -> register A2A/MCP handlers and HTTP server -> mgr.Start.

Database migrations MUST be applied before any connection/query: the resolved DB URL is computed once so migration runner and pool share the same value, migrations.RunUp runs first, and only then does database.Connect open the pool — a migration failure os.Exit(1)s the process.

The persistent data store is PostgreSQL, accessed via jackc/pgx v5 pgxpool; Connect parses the URL, optionally registers pgvector types when VectorEnabled, and retries Ping with exponential backoff (500ms initial, 5s max, 120s overall timeout) until ready.

Vector/embedding storage uses the pgvector extension: when VectorEnabled the pool registers pgvector types on each connection (pgvector-go/pgx), gated by the --database-vector-enabled flag (default true) for the memory table.

core/internal/database implements the persistence layer as a postgresClient that wraps sqlc-generated Queries (core/internal/database/gen) and the pgxpool, providing transactional helpers (withTx begin/commit/rollback) behind the api/database.Client interface.

The DB contract is split: api/database defines the storage model TYPES (Agent, Event, Session, Tool, ToolServer, Feedback, etc.) and the Client interface, while core/internal/database provides the concrete Postgres IMPLEMENTATION — the implementation imports the api package and returns dbpkg.Client, never the reverse.

core/internal/controller/reconciler.kagentReconciler is the core reconciliation engine implementing KagentReconciler: it reconciles Agents, SandboxAgents, ModelConfigs, ModelProviderConfigs, RemoteMCPServers, MCPServers and MCP services, holding the adkTranslator, kube client, dbClient, and sandboxBackend.

core/internal/controller/translator/agent translates a kagent v1alpha2 Agent CRD into Kubernetes workload objects (Deployment) and ADK config (api/adk), applying image/egress/MCP-service conventions; ValidationError marks user-fixable config errors that must NOT trigger exponential-backoff retries.

core/internal/httpserver hosts the REST API plus mounts the A2A and MCP handlers; it exposes a fixed set of /api/* paths (agents, sessions, tasks, tools, toolservers, modelconfigs, memories, feedback, namespaces, sandboxagents, agentharnesses) and infra paths (/health, /version, /mcp, /api/a2a).

core/internal/a2a.A2ARegistrar is a manager.Runnable that watches the cache and registers per-agent A2A handlers/clients, bridging the controller's reconciler+translator to the A2A protocol surface (handler mux, AgentClientRegistry, substrate sandbox actor backend).

The controller is built on the Kubernetes controller-runtime: it registers v1alpha1, v1alpha2, kmcp v1alpha1, agent-sandbox and substrate schemes, runs a Manager with leader election (LeaderElectionID 0e9f6799.kagent.dev), metrics, and health/ready probes.
HTTP/2 is disabled by default on the metrics/webhook TLS servers (NextProtos forced to http/1.1) unless --enable-http2 is set, deliberately to avoid the HTTP/2 Rapid Reset / Stream Cancellation CVEs.

api/v1alpha2 is the current CRD API group (Group kagent.dev, Version v1alpha2) where new features go; api/v1alpha1 is the legacy group kept for backward compatibility — both register into the same controller scheme.

adk/cmd/main.go is the per-agent Go ADK runtime binary: it materializes+loads agent config from a config dir, initializes telemetry, optionally starts a KAgent token+session+memory service (when KAGENT_URL is set), builds a Google-ADK Runner and KAgentExecutor, then runs an A2A HTTP server.

adk/pkg/a2a.KAgentExecutor implements the Google ADK a2asrv.AgentExecutor interface, wrapping a runner.Config, session service, and skills directory to execute agent turns over the A2A protocol; the assertion var _ a2asrv.AgentExecutor enforces the contract.

ADK agent app_name identity is derived deterministically: when both KAGENT_NAMESPACE and KAGENT_NAME env vars are set they are dash-to-underscore normalized and joined as namespace + __NS__ + name; otherwise it falls back to the agent card name, then to go-adk-agent.

The ADK runtime supports multiple LLM provider backends — Anthropic (incl. Bedrock/Vertex), OpenAI, Ollama, AWS Bedrock, SAP AI Core, and Google GenAI/Gemini — each implemented as a model adapter under adk/pkg/models.
core/cli (binary core/cli/cmd/kagent) is a Cobra-based CLI and TUI named kagent, composing agent, mcp, envdoc subcommands plus a profiles/config layer and a Bubble Tea TUI workspace.

api/client provides the Go HTTP client SDK (ClientSet) for the controller's REST API, aggregating typed sub-clients (Agent, Session, Tool, ToolServer, ModelConfig, ModelProviderConfig, Model, Memory, Namespace, Feedback, Health, Version) over a shared BaseClient; the CLI is its primary consumer.

core/cmd/skills-init/main.go is a Kubernetes init-container binary that fetches an Agent's skills from git repositories and OCI images before the main agent container starts, reading config from a ConfigMap-mounted JSON file.

skills-init performs all subprocess invocations with argv vectors and never interpolates user input into a shell — an explicit hardening invariant called out as the original design defect the rewrite eliminated (command-injection avoidance).

Database schema migrations are compiled into the binary via go:embed (embed.FS over the core and vector directories) in core/pkg/migrations, so the controller carries and applies its own migrations at startup.

core/pkg/sandboxbackend provides pluggable sandbox execution backends behind a Backend/AsyncBackend interface — agentsxk8s (default, plain Kubernetes pods), openshell (gRPC gateway) and substrate (Agent Substrate ate-api) — selected/composed at startup via NewRoutingBackend based on CLI flags.

Optional runtime integrations are gated by config presence: the Substrate AgentHarness backend is wired only when --substrate-ate-api-endpoint is set, and the OpenShell sandbox backend only when --openshell-gateway-url is set; with none set the AgentHarness controller is disabled and logged.

Cross-module dependency flows one way: core imports api (api/database, api/v1alpha2, api/adk, api/httpapi) and adk, while api imports only its own sub-packages and external libs — api never imports core, keeping shared types free of controller/server logic.

Agent-to-agent communication and tool integration use two open protocols: the A2A protocol (a2aproject/a2a-go, both v1 and v2) for invoking agents, and MCP (Model Context Protocol, modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk) for tool servers — both surfaced as HTTP handlers (/api/a2a, /mcp) by the controller and natively by the ADK runtime.

Agent runtime events (conversation history) are persisted as JSON-serialized A2A protocol messages: the Event model stores the message in a Data string field and Parse/ParseMessages round-trip it back to a2a.Message via encoding/json.

Configuration precedence is flags then environment: LoadFromEnv visits every defined flag, derives an UPPER_SNAKE env name (dashes->underscores), and overrides the flag value when that env var is non-empty.

api/openshell holds generated gRPC/protobuf client stubs (compute, datamodel, inference, sandbox, openshell test/v1 services) used by the core OpenShell sandbox backend to talk to the external OpenShell gateway.

adjudication.note: No EC states the controller listens on port 8083 as the entry point for the UI's A2A requests. ECs cover the controller binary entry (EC-002) and that A2A is surfaced (EC-029), but the specific 8083 listen port / UI-entry fact is absent. manifest_builder.go:379 confirms 8083 KAGENT_URL.

EC-013 (A2ARegistrar bridges the controller's reconciler+translator to the A2A protocol surface, registering per-agent A2A handlers/clients) plus EC-029 (A2A surfaced as /api/a2a by the controller AND natively by the ADK runtime) and EC-017 (agent pod runs the ADK Runner) together capture the controller-proxies-A2A-to-the-pod role. Fact-keyed bridge across the registrar/runtime split.
No EC mentions the http://{name}.{namespace}:8080 agent-pod Service URL. compiler.go:98 confirms the URL construction.

EC-009 names the storage model TYPES (Agent, Event, Session, Tool, ToolServer, Feedback, etc.) and EC-030 covers the Event model; EC-012 lists memories/feedback paths. models.go confirms all 8 models incl. Memory (pgvector.Vector, ExpiresAt). The model-set fact is captured.
No EC mentions the Session.Source user/agent column or the agent-sourced-session exclusion. models.go:55-77 + sessions.sql:14 confirm the invariant.

EC-003 (registers every controller) + EC-010 (the shared kagentReconciler is the core engine all controllers delegate to). app.go registers AgentController, RemoteMCPServerController, MCPServerToolController, etc., all wired to one rcnclr — confirmed.
EC-010 explicitly states the kagentReconciler holds adkTranslator, kube client, dbClient (and sandboxBackend). reconciler.go:83-124 confirms exactly these fields and the single shared instance.
No EC mentions ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE atomic upserts / lock-free idempotency. agents.sql:14 + tools.sql:41 + the mutex-free reconciler confirm the upsert invariant.

EC-008 mentions withTx helpers generically but no EC states tool refresh wraps delete-all + insert in one transaction. client_postgres.go:358-374 confirms RefreshToolsForServer runs SoftDelete + upserts inside withTx.
No EC states network I/O (MCP connect + listTools) happens outside DB transactions. reconciler.go:1072-1099 confirms the upsert/connect/list/refresh ordering with the tx only at the end.
No EC mentions the AgentController event predicate (GenerationChanged OR LabelChanged). agent_controller.go:71 confirms the For() predicate.

EC-011 translates 'a kagent v1alpha2 Agent CRD' (establishing the Agent CRD exists in v1alpha2) and EC-016 confirms the v1alpha2 group. The Declarative|BYO enum + refs detail is not enumerated by an EC, but the component-exists fact (the central Agent CRD in v1alpha2) is captured. agent_types.go:31-68 confirms the type enum + sub-specs.
No EC mentions Agent CEL XValidation (type enum, sub-spec presence, systemMessage exclusivity, serviceAccount exclusivity). agent_types.go:51-52,168,422 confirm the rules.
No EC mentions the McpServerTool requireApproval-subset-of-toolNames CEL rule. agent_types.go:533 confirms it.

EC-022/EC-012 establish ModelConfig as a resource (sub-client + /api/modelconfigs) and EC-020 enumerates the same provider set (Anthropic/OpenAI/AzureOpenAI/Ollama/Gemini/GeminiVertexAI/AnthropicVertexAI/Bedrock/SAPAICore) for the ADK runtime adapters. Fact-keyed bridge: ModelConfig configures the LLM provider over that enum. modelconfig_types.go:30 confirms the 9-value enum.

No EC mentions ModelConfig CEL rules (provider-block nil, passthrough/secret exclusivity, passthrough disallowed for Gemini/VertexAI, caCert ref/key together). modelconfig_types.go:371-385,318-319 confirm them.

No EC asserts the RemoteMCPServer CRD with protocol enum (SSE|STREAMABLE_HTTP), default, headersFrom, terminateOnClose, allowedNamespaces, or status.discoveredTools. remotemcpserver_types.go:31-119 confirms all fields.

EC-010 lists RemoteMCPServers among reconciled types but no EC captures the substantive role (connect to MCP server, list tools, populate status.discoveredTools). reconciler.go:600+/745 confirms the discovery behavior.
No EC mentions the common types ValueRef, AllowedNamespaces (From All|Selector, Gateway API pattern), TypedReference/TypedLocalReference. common_types.go:36-53,147 confirms them.

EC-011 (the translator/agent package translates the Agent CRD into ADK config api/adk). compiler.go:113-213 confirms translateInlineAgent is the CRD->AgentConfig conversion entry. Fact-keyed match (the RC's adk_api_translator.go file ref is the same package).

EC-011 mentions 'ADK config (api/adk)' but no EC captures the three-type-system pipeline (CRD Go types -> Go ADK types -> Python ADK types serialized as config.json). api/adk/types.go confirms AgentConfig/HttpMcpServerConfig/SseMcpServerConfig.
No EC mentions the config-Secret-mounted-at-/config delivery and the agent reading /config/config.json at startup. manifest_builder.go:83 + deployments.go:163 + cli.py:58,63 confirm it.
No EC mentions the text/template include() prompt-template resolution at reconcile time. template.go:97-114 + compiler.go gating confirm it.
No EC mentions the promptTemplate context variables (AgentName/AgentNamespace/Description/ToolNames/SkillNames). template.go:17-28 confirms the 5 fields.
No EC mentions the kagent-builtin-prompts Helm ConfigMap and its 5 keys. builtin-prompts-configmap.yaml confirms them (helm template, readable in the repo).
No EC mentions the controller watching ConfigMaps referenced by agents and re-reconciling dependents. agent_controller.go:78-83 confirms the configMap dependency finder.

No EC covers the Python HITL before_tool_callback / request_confirmation plumbing. _approval.py confirms it. The blind emission was Go-infra-centric and did not project the Python kagent-adk subsystem.
No EC mentions KAgentRemoteA2ATool (the BaseTool replacing AgentTool(RemoteA2aAgent)). _remote_a2a_tool.py:158 confirms it. Python subsystem not projected.
No EC mentions KAgentRemoteA2AToolset (the BaseToolset httpx cleanup wrapper). _remote_a2a_tool.py:523 confirms it.
No EC mentions the _SubagentInterceptor injecting x-user-id and x-kagent-source:agent headers. _remote_a2a_tool.py:58-97,218 confirms it.
No EC mentions A2aAgentExecutor building the SubagentSessionProvider {tool->session} map or the event converter stamping kagent_subagent_session_id. _agent_executor.py:590-595 confirms it.

scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent Mirror cycle (separation of duties): curator → blind engine (doc-blind, Graphify path) → independent scorer → adversarial refuter per deepinit_wrong.
